{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Digital Marketer / Content Creator",
  "description": "Online Foodstuff Store Limited&nbsp;is a leading retail and supply chain company dedicated to making quality foodstuffs accessible and affordable across Nigeria. Established with the mission to simplify food sourcing for households, restaurants, and businesses, the company has grown into a trusted brand recognized for its consistency, transparency, and excellent service delivery.

Headquartered in Kubwa, Abuja, with branches in Lagos and Port Harcourt, we operate a strong distribution network that ensures seamless procurement, packaging, and delivery of food items. By leveraging modern systems, efficient logistics, and vendor partnerships, we are able to meet the daily food needs of thousands of customers while maintaining high standards of quality and affordability.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketer / Content Creator

Job Location:&nbsp;Kubwa, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute digital marketing strategies
	Create engaging content for social media and online platforms
	Manage and grow the company&rsquo;s online presence
	Analyze campaign performance and optimize results


Requirements


	Proven experience in digital marketing and content creation
	Strong understanding of social media platforms and trends
	Creativity and excellent communication skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application with a link to their portfolio to:&nbsp;hr@foodstuff.store&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Digital Marketer / Content Creator Job at Online Foodstuff Store Limited




        
    

        
        Online Foodstuff Store Limited is a leading retail and supply chain company dedicated to making quality foodstuffs accessible and affordable across Nigeria. Established with the mission to simplify food sourcing for households, restaurants, and businesses, the company has grown into a trusted brand recognized for its consistency, transparency, and excellent service delivery.

Headquartered in Kubwa, Abuja, with branches in Lagos and Port Harcourt, we operate a strong distribution network that ensures seamless procurement, packaging, and delivery of food items. By leveraging modern systems, efficient logistics, and vendor partnerships, we are able to meet the daily food needs of thousands of customers while maintaining high standards of quality and affordability.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Marketer / Content Creator

Job Location: Kubwa, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute digital marketing strategies
	Create engaging content for social media and online platforms
	Manage and grow the company’s online presence
	Analyze campaign performance and optimize results


Requirements


	Proven experience in digital marketing and content creation
	Strong understanding of social media platforms and trends
	Creativity and excellent communication skills
	Ability to work independently and meet deadlines.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application with a link to their portfolio to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Compliance Manager",
  "description": "Ceviant Payment Nigeria Limited&nbsp;is a subsidiary of Ceviant Finance Limited focused on delivering cloud-based treasury and payment automation solutions for businesses engaged in domestic and international trade.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Compliance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	We are looking for a Compliance Manager to ensure that Ceviant Payments Nigeria Limited operates in full compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, and requirements applicable to a Payment Solution Service Provider (PSSP) licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria.
	The role oversees regulatory compliance, AML/CFT controls, internal policy governance, and regulatory engagement to ensure the company maintains its license and operates within approved guidelines


Role Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain the company&rsquo;s compliance policies and procedures in line with PSSP licensing requirements and regulatory guidelines.
	Serve as primary liaison during CBN examinations.
	Monitor regulatory developments from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), and other relevant bodies.
	Prepare and submit weekly, monthly, and annual filings to regulatory authorities.
	Provide periodic compliance reports to: Managing Director, Board of Directors (where applicable)
	Conduct compliance risk assessments, identifying gaps and recommending mitigating controls.
	Review new and existing products, services, and business processes to ensure regulatory alignment.
	Liaise with regulatory bodies and ensure a timely response to queries.
	Implement compliance policies and procedures.
	Oversee AML/CFT monitoring activities and reporting, including suspicious transaction reporting.
	Ensure merchants and partners comply with onboarding standards.
	Conduct compliance reviews of merchant agreements.
	Monitor chargeback risks, fraud indicators, and transaction anomalies.
	Recommend risk mitigation measures.
	Maintain the company&rsquo;s compliance records and documentation for internal audits and regulatory inspections.
	Provide training and guidance to staff on compliance obligations, policies, and procedures.
	Coordinate with internal stakeholders and external regulators during audits or inquiries.
	Support the management team in implementing risk mitigation strategies.


Educational Qualification &amp; Work Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Law, Finance, Accounting, or related fields
	Minimum of 9 years of compliance experience, preferably in fintech, payments, or banking.
	Professional certifications (e.g., ACAMS, ICA, or equivalent) preferred.
	Strong understanding of Nigerian financial regulations, including PSSP licensing, CBN guidelines, AML/CFT regulations, and electronic payments.
	Experience in regulatory reporting, risk assessments, and internal audits.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and ability to handle sensitive information
	Familiarity with local and international regulatory frameworks is an advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of CBN regulations, especially PSSP licensing requirements
	Working understanding of AML/CFT frameworks, KYC, transaction monitoring, and STR/SAR reporting
	Familiarity with payment systems, electronic transactions, and settlement processes.
	Knowledge of the Nigerian Data Protection Act (NDPA) and data privacy requirements.
	Ability to interpret and implement regulatory guidelines, circulars, and directives.
	Experience supporting regulatory audits, examinations, and reporting
	Ability to liaise with regulators, banks, auditors, and internal teams.


Why Join Ceviant?


	Work in a dynamic, innovative, and collaborative environment.
	Opportunity to shape compliance and governance frameworks in a regulated PSSP environment.
	Flexible Working Arrangement
	Competitive Compensation package.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates are invited to send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@ceviant.com&nbsp;using &quot;Compliance Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-28",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-29",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Compliance Manager Job at Ceviant Payment Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Ceviant Payment Nigeria Limited is a subsidiary of Ceviant Finance Limited focused on delivering cloud-based treasury and payment automation solutions for businesses engaged in domestic and international trade.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Compliance Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a Compliance Manager to ensure that Ceviant Payments Nigeria Limited operates in full compliance with all relevant laws, regulations, and requirements applicable to a Payment Solution Service Provider (PSSP) licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria.
	The role oversees regulatory compliance, AML/CFT controls, internal policy governance, and regulatory engagement to ensure the company maintains its license and operates within approved guidelines


Role Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and maintain the company’s compliance policies and procedures in line with PSSP licensing requirements and regulatory guidelines.
	Serve as primary liaison during CBN examinations.
	Monitor regulatory developments from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), and other relevant bodies.
	Prepare and submit weekly, monthly, and annual filings to regulatory authorities.
	Provide periodic compliance reports to: Managing Director, Board of Directors (where applicable)
	Conduct compliance risk assessments, identifying gaps and recommending mitigating controls.
	Review new and existing products, services, and business processes to ensure regulatory alignment.
	Liaise with regulatory bodies and ensure a timely response to queries.
	Implement compliance policies and procedures.
	Oversee AML/CFT monitoring activities and reporting, including suspicious transaction reporting.
	Ensure merchants and partners comply with onboarding standards.
	Conduct compliance reviews of merchant agreements.
	Monitor chargeback risks, fraud indicators, and transaction anomalies.
	Recommend risk mitigation measures.
	Maintain the company’s compliance records and documentation for internal audits and regulatory inspections.
	Provide training and guidance to staff on compliance obligations, policies, and procedures.
	Coordinate with internal stakeholders and external regulators during audits or inquiries.
	Support the management team in implementing risk mitigation strategies.


Educational Qualification & Work Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Law, Finance, Accounting, or related fields
	Minimum of 9 years of compliance experience, preferably in fintech, payments, or banking.
	Professional certifications (e.g., ACAMS, ICA, or equivalent) preferred.
	Strong understanding of Nigerian financial regulations, including PSSP licensing, CBN guidelines, AML/CFT regulations, and electronic payments.
	Experience in regulatory reporting, risk assessments, and internal audits.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
	High level of integrity, attention to detail, and ability to handle sensitive information
	Familiarity with local and international regulatory frameworks is an advantage.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of CBN regulations, especially PSSP licensing requirements
	Working understanding of AML/CFT frameworks, KYC, transaction monitoring, and STR/SAR reporting
	Familiarity with payment systems, electronic transactions, and settlement processes.
	Knowledge of the Nigerian Data Protection Act (NDPA) and data privacy requirements.
	Ability to interpret and implement regulatory guidelines, circulars, and directives.
	Experience supporting regulatory audits, examinations, and reporting
	Ability to liaise with regulators, banks, auditors, and internal teams.


Why Join Ceviant?


	Work in a dynamic, innovative, and collaborative environment.
	Opportunity to shape compliance and governance frameworks in a regulated PSSP environment.
	Flexible Working Arrangement
	Competitive Compensation package.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates are invited to send their CV to: [email protected] using "Compliance Manager" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Elizabeth Maddeux Limited - Our client, a leading construction, manufacturing, and industrial distribution group, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Executive Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	Our client is seeking a highly organized and commercially aware Executive Assistant to provide strategic, administrative, and operational support to the CEO.
	The ideal candidate will possess strong experience supporting senior executives within construction, engineering or manufacturing environments.
	The Executive Assistant will play a critical role in coordinating executive priorities across engineering, project management, commercial sales, and marketing functions.


Key Responsibilities


	Executive Office &amp; Calendar Management
	Executive Communication &amp; Stakeholder Coordination
	Strategic &amp; Commercial Support
	Meeting &amp; Project Coordination
	Executive Office Operations


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Engineering Management, Marketing, or related discipline.
	4 &ndash; 8 years of experience supporting senior executives, preferably within construction, engineering, manufacturing, or industrial distribution companies.
	Experience working in a project-driven or engineering-oriented organization is strongly preferred.
	Exposure to commercial operations, sales coordination, or marketing support within technical industries is an added advantage.
	Proven ability to operate effectively within a fast-paced executive environment with multiple stakeholders.


Technical Skills:


	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Strong report writing, executive documentation, and presentation development skills.
	Experience using project management tools, CRM platforms, and scheduling systems.


Soft Skills:


	Strong organizational, planning, and multitasking abilities.
	High emotional intelligence and stakeholder management capability.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Core Competencies:


	Project and operational coordination
	Commercial awareness (sales &amp; market dynamics)
	Strategic thinking and initiative
	High attention to detail and accuracy
	Confidentiality and discretion.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@elizabethmaddeux.com&nbsp;using &quot;Executive Assistant&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Senior Executive Assistant Job at Elizabeth Maddeux Limited




        
    

        
        Elizabeth Maddeux Limited - Our client, a leading construction, manufacturing, and industrial distribution group, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Executive Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Our client is seeking a highly organized and commercially aware Executive Assistant to provide strategic, administrative, and operational support to the CEO.
	The ideal candidate will possess strong experience supporting senior executives within construction, engineering or manufacturing environments.
	The Executive Assistant will play a critical role in coordinating executive priorities across engineering, project management, commercial sales, and marketing functions.


Key Responsibilities


	Executive Office & Calendar Management
	Executive Communication & Stakeholder Coordination
	Strategic & Commercial Support
	Meeting & Project Coordination
	Executive Office Operations


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Management, Engineering Management, Marketing, or related discipline.
	4 – 8 years of experience supporting senior executives, preferably within construction, engineering, manufacturing, or industrial distribution companies.
	Experience working in a project-driven or engineering-oriented organization is strongly preferred.
	Exposure to commercial operations, sales coordination, or marketing support within technical industries is an added advantage.
	Proven ability to operate effectively within a fast-paced executive environment with multiple stakeholders.


Technical Skills:


	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Strong report writing, executive documentation, and presentation development skills.
	Experience using project management tools, CRM platforms, and scheduling systems.


Soft Skills:


	Strong organizational, planning, and multitasking abilities.
	High emotional intelligence and stakeholder management capability.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work independently with minimal supervision in high-pressure environments.


Core Competencies:


	Project and operational coordination
	Commercial awareness (sales & market dynamics)
	Strategic thinking and initiative
	High attention to detail and accuracy
	Confidentiality and discretion.


Salary
N400,000 - N500,000 monthly. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Executive Assistant" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Support Officer",
  "description": "Flexi Microfinance Bank (FlexiMFB) is a subsidiary of PalmPay, supporting financial services and operational expansion across Nigeria. As part of our continued growth, we are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Support Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Operations Support Officer to support field operations, ensure accurate data tracking, and drive efficient coordination across teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Support and coordinate daily activities of field agents
	Track operational data (activations, onboarding, reports)
	Maintain accurate records and prepare simple performance reports
	Ensure field teams follow processes and meet targets
	Assist in resolving field and customer-related issues
	Communicate updates between field teams and management


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in a related field
	0&ndash;2 years experience in operations, admin, or coordination role
	Must be available to resume immediately
	Basic knowledge of Excel or Google Sheets
	Good communication and problem-solving skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;otumunye.flourish@flexi-bank.com&nbsp;using&nbsp;&ldquo;Operations Support Officer&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-29",

Operations Support Officer Job at Flexi Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        Flexi Microfinance Bank (FlexiMFB) is a subsidiary of PalmPay, supporting financial services and operational expansion across Nigeria. As part of our continued growth, we are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Support Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Operations Support Officer to support field operations, ensure accurate data tracking, and drive efficient coordination across teams.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Support and coordinate daily activities of field agents
	Track operational data (activations, onboarding, reports)
	Maintain accurate records and prepare simple performance reports
	Ensure field teams follow processes and meet targets
	Assist in resolving field and customer-related issues
	Communicate updates between field teams and management


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in a related field
	0–2 years experience in operations, admin, or coordination role
	Must be available to resume immediately
	Basic knowledge of Excel or Google Sheets
	Good communication and problem-solving skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Operations Support Officer” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Electrical Engineer",
  "description": "Sterling Oil Exploration &amp; Energy Production Company Limited (SEEPCO)&nbsp;was established in 2003 and headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria, is one of the fastest-growing oil exploration and production companies in Africa. With operations in multiple countries and expanding.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer
Job Location:&nbsp;Akwa Ibom&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full Time

Purpose of The Role


	To assist lead electrical in execution of construction activities at site for the LNG Liquefaction, Storage and Export terminal. monitoring, management, executing and approving construction activities at site through selected EPC Contractors/ Contractors.


Overall, Role Description / Required Skill Sets


	Assist Lead-Electrical in planning, monitoring and execution of all electrical works associated with the project including Installation of PDB&rsquo;s, earthing work, Cable laying work, monitoring all electrical safety requirements, supervising the testing of DB&rsquo;s, commissioning and energizing of DB&rsquo;s.
	Supervision, coordination, and inspection of DBs and electrical material received at site and preparing the data&rsquo;s such as allocation of DB&rsquo;s, distribution of power to individual agencies and monitoring all safety aspects required for smooth electrical power distribution.

Electrical Engineer Job at Sterling Oil Exploration & Energy Production Company Limited




        
    

        
        Sterling Oil Exploration & Energy Production Company Limited (SEEPCO) was established in 2003 and headquartered in Lagos, Nigeria, is one of the fastest-growing oil exploration and production companies in Africa. With operations in multiple countries and expanding.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Electrical Engineer
Job Location: Akwa Ibom 
Employment Type: Full Time

Purpose of The Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To assist lead electrical in execution of construction activities at site for the LNG Liquefaction, Storage and Export terminal. monitoring, management, executing and approving construction activities at site through selected EPC Contractors/ Contractors.


Overall, Role Description / Required Skill Sets


	Assist Lead-Electrical in planning, monitoring and execution of all electrical works associated with the project including Installation of PDB’s, earthing work, Cable laying work, monitoring all electrical safety requirements, supervising the testing of DB’s, commissioning and energizing of DB’s.
	Supervision, coordination, and inspection of DBs and electrical material received at site and preparing the data’s such as allocation of DB’s, distribution of power to individual agencies and monitoring all safety aspects required for smooth electrical power distribution.
	Coordination with NG Operator / in charge and inform & record the condition for any power outage issue required for smooth construction power availability.
	Supervision, coordination, and inspection of entire electrical facilities, preparing of periodic maintenance schedule and inventory list required for smooth operation of all electrical systems
	Supervision, Coordination, and inspection of electrical system by using electrical measuring and troubleshooting instruments such as Insulation Tester, Earth Resistance Tester, ELCB tester etc.
	Checking of plans, quantities for its accuracy as per drawing.
	Preparing the list of material required for smooth distribution of construction
	Preparing a daily progress report, muster register.
	Plan, monitor and schedule the material quantities as per project requirements.
	Contractors / EPC to ensure compliance with established HSE policies, procedures, quality, contract, and schedule requirements.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the email.
